content.problemStatement:
Dream abstracts the fabric of spacetime as a directed rooted tree (arborescence) with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). Node 111 is the root and for each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N‚ąí11 \le i \le N-11‚Č§i‚Č§N‚ąí1), the parent of node i+1i+1i+1 is fif_ifi‚Äč. All edges of this tree are directed away from the root.

Then, Dream employs a magical superpower and adds MMM directed edges to this tree in such a way that the resulting directed graph remains acyclic (a DAG).

Let's call a node of this DAG an event and further call a simple path on this DAG an era. Dream considers a pair of events (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) to be plausible if there is an era whose first event is iii and last event is jjj. Note that i<ji \lt ji<j does not have to hold for a plausible pair.

Dream now wants you to answer QQQ queries. In each query, he gives you two positive integers lll and rrr, where l‚Č§rl \leq rl‚Č§r, and he wishes to know the number of plausible pairs of events (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that l‚Č§i<j‚Č§rl \leq i \lt j \leq rl‚Č§i<j‚Č§r.
constraints: 1‚Č§fi‚Č§N1 \le f_i \le N1‚Č§fi‚Äč‚Č§N for each valid iii
the graph described on the input is acyclic

Subtasks**Subtask #1 (10 points):** the graph is a line ‚Äē that is, M=0M = 0M=0 and there is a permutation p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1,p_2,\ldots,p_Np1‚Äč,p2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äč such that p1=1p_1=1p1‚Äč=1 and nodes p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1,p_2,\ldots,p_Np1‚Äč,p2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äč form a directed path in the tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 space-separated integers f1,f2,‚Ä¶,fN‚ąí1f_1, f_2, \ldots, f_{N-1}f1‚Äč,f2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,fN‚ąí1‚Äč.
MMM lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv describing an additional edge from node uuu to node vvv.
The following line contains a single integer QQQ.
QQQ lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers lll and rrr describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the number of plausible pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that l‚Č§i<j‚Č§rl \leq i \lt j \leq rl‚Č§i<j‚Č§r.
